What is Sidekick?

An agentic interface for Distyl.

  • Agentic You work through an agent, not the UI.
  • Abstracts complexity No need to know which routine to edit, or what's active in production.
  • General-purpose + specific A capable agent, plus skills tuned to our work.
02
But why?

200K customer calls a day.

  • The teams
    Care, consumer, product, and design couldn't use Distillery directly. Many had never used an AI tool before.
  • The knowledge gap
    The people who knew what good looked like had no path to change what was in production. Everything went through a developer.
  • The workaround
    Our coding agents team hit the same wall. distyl-scripts was the repo they built to store what the platform didn't surface.
03
What is Sidekick?

Skills encode
repeatable workflows.

Build
  • Edit a routine
  • Create evals
  • Build a feature
Fix
  • RCA a defect
  • Reproduce an issue
  • Fix an issue
Monitor
  • Run prod analysis
  • Pull eval results
  • Query Databricks
In production Sam, a conversation designer, used them to ship a feature end-to-end.
04
How It Works

One session. Every tool.

Sidekick
agent-chat
Toolkit app
+
agent-runner
Creates a fresh sandbox per session
Skills
Context Mesh
Loaded at session start
AI systems
Distillery
Routines, proposals, evals
called on demand
Connectors
External tools
Your credentials
W&B Weave · Jira · Databricks
05
Sidekick usage

Four weeks.

832
Sidekick sessions
52
Unique users
#1
Largest cohort
75%
of proposals created
with Sidekick
06
Safety

Guardrails are built
into Distillery.

Branching
Edits happen on a branch — never directly on what's live. Nothing touches production until it's ready.
Versioning
Every change is a saved version. Anything that slips through can be rolled back instantly.
Evals
Automated tests run before anything merges. No regressions ship without being caught first.
Approval
Sidekick can't merge its own changes. Every proposal needs a second set of eyes before it ships.
The process: User request → Proposal → Evals pass → Human approves → Merge
06
Sidekick demo
07

06
Appendix

How we got here.

Jan 2026
distyl-scripts
Code agents team built repo on highside.
May 2026
Moves to Context Mesh
Austin + Eric formalize it. Rohan names it Sidekick — after Tower's phone.
May 28
Deployed highside
Rachel + Eric ship it. Bug bash same day. P0s found and fixed.
Early June
Distyl Academy
First real users — Tower interns, Seattle onsite. ~20 people.
June 17
Sidekick Academy
40 non-engineering Tower stakeholders. Went down that morning — back up in time.
Now
832 sessions
Tower is the largest cohort. Embedded Sidekick in design review.
A1
Appendix

What's architecturally interesting.

01
No shared credentials
Your keys, your account. mitmproxy injects per-PID — enforced by the kernel, not application logic. Nothing stored server-side.
02
Skills are just a git repo
Markdown files in Context Mesh. Versioning, branching, diffs, rollback — for free. The same review process that guards code guards skills.
03
Model is swappable
agent-runner abstracts the runtime — Claude SDK or OpenCode. Skills, credential proxy, sandbox: none of it is coupled to Claude.
04
Ephemeral sandbox → durable output
Session is disposable. The proposal it creates lands in Distillery with an approval gate. Mistakes are catchable before they reach main.
A2
